extraction.one_line_summary: HIGUITA Sergio attacked solo on penultimate Cat-1 climb, dropped POGAČAR and QUINTANA, won by 15 seconds over ROGLIČ group.
tactical_patterns: HIGUITA attacked repeatedly on final climbs after mid-season transfer, exploiting GC riders marking each other not new threats.
LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel prioritized staying with POGAČAR/QUINTANA group over chasing stage, correctly calculating white jersey mathematics via time gaps.
FUGLSANG Jakob attacked GC group multiple times but QUINTANA/LÓPEZ/POGAČAR marked each other allowing aggressive non-GC rider to escape.
ROGLIČ Primož defended red jersey by relying on KUSS Sepp pacing rather than team numbers, showing Jumbo depth compensates isolation.
POGAČAR dropped QUINTANA on final climb despite being only 20, revealing Grand Tour endurance superiority over three-week veterans.

rider_signals.HIGUITA Sergio: positive: mid-season signing from third-division team won stage 18 with solo long-range attack, extraordinary adaptation speed
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: positive: tactically mature to stay with rivals rather than chase stage, reclaimed white jersey via calculated 59-second gain
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: dropped QUINTANA on final climb in first Grand Tour at age 20, moved to fifth overall despite losing white jersey
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: dropped from second to third overall after being distanced by POGAČAR, lost podium position momentum from stage 17 gains
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: neutral: attacked repeatedly but failed to stick moves, suggesting GC legs present but finishing punch absent
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: defended 2:50 lead with only KUSS as support, no panic when isolated unlike typical GC race leader behavior
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: sole Jumbo support for ROGLIČ on final climbs, pacing effectiveness compensated for team numerical disadvantage
rider_signals.KNOX James: positive: held top-10 GC position despite team being reduced to three riders early in race, exceptional survival composure
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: remained third overall but showed no attacking initiative when HIGUITA escaped, defensive podium tactics prioritized
rider_signals.HAGEN Carl Fredrik: positive: first Grand Tour, helped pace QUINTANA group on final climb showing domestique value beyond expectations
